pattern: Because a single node is always a valid path, the cheapest path from any chosen root is just that root's own price, so the real work is finding, for every possible root at once, the priciest path that bends away from it.
hints: The minimum-price path starting at any root is always the root by itself — a lone node is a legal path, and nothing shorter exists, so there is no separate 'minimum' calculation to do.

For a fixed root the maximum-price path is a straight descent into the deepest-priced branch beneath it; if you also let the path bend at an interior node between two different branches, one traversal from any single starting point can evaluate every node as though it were the root.

At each node during a post-order walk, track the best downward branch that still counts its own far end and the best downward branch with its far end's price already stripped out; combining one of each kind across two different children at every node, and taking the overall best such combination, gives the answer without ever re-rooting the tree.

edge_inputs.description: a single isolated node with no edges at all
the smallest possible tree, two nodes joined by one edge
a star graph, so the answer must come from combining the two priciest leaves only
one node has three children; only the best two of the three branches may be combined, not all of them
a long unbranching chain, so the best path is the full straight descent
one node's price sits far above every other, isolating where the maximum path must pass through
every node shares the same price, so ties between candidate paths must resolve consistently

stress.description: randomly shaped trees built by attaching each new node to a uniformly chosen earlier node, with varied prices
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([parent, i])
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    price = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [n, edges, price]

solutions.python:
from collections import defaultdict
import sys

class Solution:
    def maxOutput(self, n, edges, price):
        sys.setrecursionlimit(300000)
        graph = defaultdict(list)
        for a, b in edges:
            graph[a].append(b)
            graph[b].append(a)
        ans = 0

        def dfs(node, parent):
            nonlocal ans
            max1 = price[node]
            max2 = 0
            for child in graph[node]:
                if child == parent:
                    continue
                c1, c2 = dfs(child, node)
                ans = max(ans, max1 + c2, max2 + c1)
                max1 = max(max1, c1 + price[node])
                max2 = max(max2, c2 + price[node])
            return max1, max2

        dfs(0, -1)
        return ans
